Title: Population genomics identifies genetic signatures of carrot domestication and improvement and uncovers the origin of high-carotenoid orange carrots

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: DH1; Lunar White compared for locus structure
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 630-accession diversity panel; DH1 doubled haploid orange Nantes type for reference genome
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: EXECUTER1-like protein; partial homology to Arabidopsis EXECUTER1
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recessive cultivated allele promotes high α- and β-carotene accumulation and orange root phenotype; essential with Or for high-carotenoid orange carrots
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DCAR_730022 is the strongest candidate gene for the Y2 locus on chromosome 7. 2. It is downregulated in orange genotypes carrying the recessive Y2 allele. 3. A Helitron insertion disrupts its coding sequence in DH1 and 97% of orange accessions. 4. Allelic analysis shows the recessive cultivated allele significantly increases the α+β-carotene fraction and is required, together with Or, for high-carotenoid orange roots.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; fine-mapping overlap with Y2; RNA-seq differential expression; structural variant/causal mutation analysis; allelic effect and epistasis analysis; transcriptional interactome network analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Downregulated in orange genotypes carrying the recessive Y2 allele relative to yellow genotypes with the wild allele
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in orange roots carrying cultivated Y2 allele
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing (BioProject PRJNA865653 under umbrella PRJNA285926); RNA-seq for Y2 analysis from BioProject PRJNA350691; metabolite profiling by HPLC
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a key link between photosystem II light-harvesting/singlet oxygen response and isoprenoid biosynthetic pathways. Proposed mechanism is that loss of EX1-like function may maintain high β-carotene biosynthesis for singlet oxygen quenching; functional validation still needed.

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: DH1 and segregating Or mapping population
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 630-accession diversity panel; orange/yellow segregating mapping population
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recessive cultivated allele at Or promotes orange root color and high α- and β-carotene accumulation; essential with Y2/EX1-like for high-carotenoid orange carrots
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DCAR_310369 was confirmed as the gene controlling the Or locus on chromosome 3. 2. It is the only carotenoid-related candidate within the peak associated interval. 3. The recessive allele corresponding to DcOR3Leu is associated with increased α+β-carotene and orange roots. 4. Prior knockdown evidence cited in the paper showed reduced expression converts orange carrot to yellow, while this study found no differential expression between Or alleles, suggesting protein-level control.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS colocalization; positional candidate prioritization; RNA-seq comparison; allelic effect and epistasis analysis; prior functional knockdown evidence integrated by authors
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not differentially expressed between yellow carrots carrying dominant allele and orange carrots carrying recessive allele
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not differentially expressed in compared root samples differing at Or
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing (BioProject PRJNA865653 under umbrella PRJNA285926); RNA-seq for Or analysis (BioProject PRJNA865166 under umbrella PRJNA285926); metabolite profiling by HPLC
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ELIP1 (interaction referenced from prior knowledge discussed in paper)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The recessive allele associated here corresponds to the leucine-coding DcOR3Leu allele previously proven to control orange phenotype. Function likely regulated at protein level rather than transcript abundance.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: REC1-like protein; homologous to Arabidopsis REDUCED CHLOROPLAST COVERAGE 1
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recessive cultivated allele at the REC1 locus contributes to the highest α- and β-carotene accumulation and enhances orange phenotype in combination with Or and EX1-like
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DCAR_206039 marks a newly identified chromosome 2 locus associated with carotenoid accumulation. 2. It is the leading candidate for the REC1 locus based on positional and functional homology evidence. 3. The recessive cultivated allele significantly increases α+β-carotene proportion. 4. REC1 is not strictly sufficient alone for orange roots but contributes to the highest carotenoid levels when combined with recessive Or and EX1-like alleles.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; positional candidate analysis; single-marker effect analysis; epistasis/allelic interaction analysis
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing (BioProject PRJNA865653 under umbrella PRJNA285926); metabolite profiling by HPLC
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named here as REC1 candidate locus; functional validation remains to be completed. Evidence includes homology to Arabidopsis REC1 and similarity to Mimulus RCP2-related carotenoid effects.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 630-accession diversity panel; orange/yellow segregating mapping population
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not differentially expressed between yellow and orange genotypes at Or
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with plastid biogenesis/chloroplast-associated machinery; interaction with ELIP1 discussed from prior evidence
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Carotenoid accumulation via regulation of chromoplast biogenesis and interaction between carotenoid biosynthesis and photosynthetic system
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Light-response/chloroplast biogenesis related regulation suggested; specific feedback loop not directly demonstrated in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Carotenoid biosynthetic pathway; plastid biogenesis modules
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes high α- and β-carotene accumulation and orange taproot when recessive cultivated allele is present
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key target for marker-assisted breeding of orange high-provitamin A carrots; should be combined with recessive EX1/Y2 and favorable REC1 allele for highest carotenoid content
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Plastid development regulator / Orange family protein
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing PRJNA865653; RNA-seq PRJNA865166; HPLC metabolite phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Confirmed as the causal gene for Or. 2. Recessive cultivated allele is associated with orange phenotype and increased α+β-carotene. 3. Or and EX1 cultivated alleles are both required for high-carotenoid orange roots. 4. Regulation likely occurs at protein/plastid level rather than transcript abundance.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential determinant of orange carrot phenotype and high carotenoid accumulation together with Y2/EX1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: No significant differential expression between allele classes in compared roots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS colocalization; RNA-seq; allele effect analysis; epistasis analysis; previous knockdown evidence cited
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a transcription factor, it is a major regulator of carotenoid accumulation and was under improvement selection.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Lower expression in orange genotypes with recessive Y2 allele
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cross-talk between singlet oxygen response/photosystem II repair-related processes and carotenoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible homeostatic coupling between singlet oxygen quenching demand and β-carotene biosynthesis proposed, but not directly proven
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Genes in photosystem II associated light-harvesting complex and isoprenoid biosynthetic pathways identified in interactome context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function/recessive allele is associated with orange roots and increased α+β-carotene
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for marker-assisted breeding at Y2; should be pyramided with Or and REC1 favorable alleles
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Chloroplast singlet oxygen response regulator / EXECUTER1-like protein
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing PRJNA865653; Y2 RNA-seq PRJNA350691; HPLC metabolite phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Singlet oxygen (^1O2); photosystem II photooxidative stress
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as the best candidate for Y2. 2. Orange carrots commonly carry a Helitron-disrupted coding sequence and reduced expression. 3. Recessive allele is essential with Or for high α+β-carotene orange phenotype. 4. Proposed to affect carotenoid accumulation through chloroplast stress/light-response signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential recessive determinant of high-carotenoid orange carrots at the Y2 locus
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in orange roots with cultivated allele
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Likely chloroplast stress signaling / loss of EXECUTER-like function altering carotenoid homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; RNA-seq; structural variant analysis; allelic effect and epistasis analysis; network analysis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional validation is still pending, but evidence strongly supports candidacy.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Plastid/chloroplast development linked to carotenoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recessive allele enhances carotenoid accumulation and contributes to strongest orange/high-carotenoid phenotype together with Or and EX1-like recessive alleles
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as an additional locus to maximize provitamin A carotenoid content when stacked with Or and Y2 favorable alleles
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing PRJNA865653; HPLC metabolite phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: REC1-like; homologous to Arabidopsis REDUCED CHLOROPLAST COVERAGE 1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as candidate gene for new REC1 locus on chromosome 2. 2. Recessive allele significantly raises α+β-carotene fraction. 3. It is not strictly required alone for orange color but boosts accumulation to highest levels in combination with Or and EX1-like. 4. Supports model linking plastid development with carotenoid storage in carrot roots.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Modifier/enhancer required for maximal α+β-carotene accumulation in orange carrots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; allele effect analysis; epistasis analysis; comparative functional inference
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional validation remains pending; identified from a new locus named REC1 in this study.
